title: phlox kelseyi var. collina
intro: Phlox kelseyi var. collina is a variety of the Kelsey's phlox, a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The plant's roots and leaves contain salicylic acid, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, similar to aspirin. Herbal preparations of Phlox kelseyi var. collina can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and sore throats.
